pullRequest.title: Fixed: Order inference servers so it defaults to the last one used
pullRequest.body:
## Issue
Order inference servers choose the default whenever moving into the foundation dashboard.
Fixes #329 
Fixes #284 

## Analysis and findings

Inference settings are in the state object called `inferenceSettings` in the RunModelButton.tsx file
Whenever the `experimentInfo` changes, there is an effect hook that runs, updating the `experimentInfo`
Line 59: if an experiment info contains the `inferenceParams`, then the whole  `inferenceSettings` state is updated.
Line 65: If the `inferenceEngine` attribute in the `inferenceSettings` is null, choose the default settings in an async method. This is where the issue happens.
Because React setState is asynchronous, and two asynchronous call operations run in parallel, the final asynchronous update is unpredictable. 

## Solution
Move the retrieving default information into a new async function `getDefaultinferenceEngines`
Assign 'experimentInfo.config.inferenceParams' to a local variable called 'objExperimentInfo'. (make synchronous) 
If 'objExperimentInfo' is null, then we will update the default settings.

## Sivierity 
Moderately, the user interface inference engine selection component is affected.

## Further Information
Suggestions for reducing prop drilling through components

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1c3a1d0-60ad-4f72-a5ae-e19ad39cd972

This change improves UX so when fields are invalid on submit, it will change to the correct tab + scroll to the invalid field. 

Before: When clicking save on the form with invalid fields, the form wouldn't scroll to/switch to the correct tab to show the invalid field. This led to a confusing UX where it appeared the save button was not doing anything.

If this issue exists in other forms using tabs within the app, it might be worth investigating if there is a universal way to apply this to those forms.

fix(computer): guard pythonLibraries before filtering
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Fixes a runtime crash on the Computer page caused by calling `.filter()` on `pythonLibraries` before verifying that the API response is an array.

## Root cause
`useAPI('server', ['pythonLibraries'])` can return non-array values (e.g. `undefined` or an object) during initial load. The UI assumed an array and called `.filter()` unconditionally, resulting in:
```
TypeError: pythonLibraries.filter is not a function
```

## Changes
- Guard `pythonLibraries` with `Array.isArray` and normalize to an empty array
- Prevent `.filter()` from running on invalid data
- Add a missing React `key` to table rows

## Screenshots / Logs
N/A — this PR fixes a runtime error and does not introduce visual changes.

## Testing
- `npm install`
- `npm start`
- Verified that the Computer → Python Libraries tab no longer crashes on initial load
- Confirmed no `TypeError` is thrown when `pythonLibraries` is undefined or non-array

## Risk & Rollback
- Low risk (frontend-only defensive check)
- Revert by reverting this commit if needed

## Related
Closes #1062

pullRequest.body:
- This remove foreign key joined relationships on user table
- This works because we have our custom `get_by_oauth_account` which does the join manually the only time when we actually need it. So we actually never needed oauth_accounts list in the user table in the first place
- This also removes all `unique()` conditions we had to add in multiple places because of thejoin
- This fixes a missing thing when creating an oauth account to verify if user exists
- This also fixes a bug when creating an oauth account with user not exists
- This deals with everything we were facing with #1000 
- Fixes #1171

Fix excessive disk usage from redundant .cache dirs in local provider runs
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Set `HF_HOME` and `XDG_CACHE_HOME` to the real user's cache directories when launching local provider jobs, so Hugging Face models and other cached assets are shared across runs instead of being re-downloaded into each job's remapped `HOME`.
- `UV_CACHE_DIR` was already shared; this closes the gap for HF and XDG caches.
- Uses `setdefault` so explicit user overrides via `config.env_vars` still take precedence.

Fixes #1604

## Test plan
- [ ] Trigger multiple local provider runs and verify that `local_provider_runs/<job_id>/workspace/.cache` is no longer created with large HF model files
- [ ] Confirm HF models are read from the host's `~/.cache/huggingface`
- [ ] Verify that setting `HF_HOME` explicitly in env_vars still overrides the default

pullRequest.title: Fix stop button for local interactive tasks (#1655)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Fix the issue where interactive tasks (like Ollama + Gradio) would hang forever when the stop button is clicked.

## Problem
- Stop button worked correctly for REMOTE jobs (called provider stop_cluster)
- Stop button failed for LOCAL jobs (only set stop flag, never killed processes)
- Interactive tasks run servers that don't exit until interrupted
- tfl-remote-trap waits for command completion, causing infinite hang

## Solution
Modified `handleStopJob` in `JobProgress.tsx` to call provider stop_cluster for ANY job with `provider_id` and `cluster_name` in job_data, regardless of job.type.

Local interactive tasks have this metadata and can now be properly terminated via LocalProvider.stop_cluster(), which sends SIGTERM to the process tree.

## Changes
- src/renderer/components/Experiment/Tasks/JobProgress.tsx: Updated handleStopJob logic

## Testing
Tested that local interactive tasks now shut down properly when stopped.

Fixes #1655

pullRequest.title: Fix name field in add provider modal to show as required
Fix misleading 'check your email' message when EMAIL_METHOD is dev
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1682 

When `EMAIL_METHOD` is `"dev"` (the default), verification emails are only logged to the console — never actually sent. But the UI says "check your email" and the console prints `✅ Verification email sent`, which misleads users into waiting for an email that never arrives.

- **Backend**: Console now prints `📋 Verification link logged to console above (as you're in dev mode)` instead of the incorrect `✅ Verification email sent` when in dev mode
- **Frontend**: Added a note to both the registration confirmation and login error messages: "If running in dev mode, check the server logs for the verification link"

fix(cli,api): give `lab job task-logs` a one-shot JSON endpoint
pullRequest.body:
Closes #1873.

## Summary

\`lab job task-logs\` was calling \`/stream_output\`, which is an **open-ended SSE tail** ([\`watch_file\`](api/transformerlab/routers/serverinfo.py) → \`text/event-stream\`). The server never closes the connection, so the CLI's \`httpx.get(timeout=15.0)\` always hit a read timeout, and even if it hadn't, \`response.json()\` would have failed on SSE-formatted frames. \`--follow\` mode was silently broken by the same mismatch, masked by a bare \`except: pass\` in the poll loop.

Fix: add a one-shot JSON sibling endpoint and point the CLI at it.

- **API:** new \`GET /experiment/{id}/jobs/{job_id}/task_logs\` that returns \`{\"logs\": \"<text>\", \"tail_lines\": N}\`. Mirrors \`/provider_logs\` exactly so the CLI's existing \`_print_logs\` / \`_stream_logs_generic\` helpers work without changes. Supports \`tail_lines\` (100–2000, default 400) and \`sweeps=true\` for parity with \`/stream_output\`.
- **CLI:** \`fetch_task_logs\` now hits \`/task_logs\` instead of \`/stream_output\`. One-line change.
- **Leaves \`/stream_output\` (SSE) untouched** — the frontend still uses it for live tailing.
- Fixes both one-shot AND \`--follow\` mode on the CLI side in a single change.

## Test plan

- [x] \`cd cli && python -m pytest tests/commands/test_job.py -v\` — 22 passed (new test \`test_task_logs_hits_task_logs_endpoint\` asserts the URL)
- [x] \`cd api && pytest test/api/test_experiment_jobs.py -v\` — 8 passed (new assertions in \`test_job_output_endpoints\` cover happy path, tail_lines param, validation)
- [x] \`ruff check\` + \`ruff format --check\` on all four changed files — clean
- [ ] Manual: \`lab --format json job task-logs <id>\` returns JSON instead of timing out
- [ ] Manual: \`lab job task-logs <id> --follow\` shows new lines as the job writes them

## Context

Discovered by \`scripts/test-task-placement.py\` (landed in #1870) — every completed job's RAW LOGS block showed the 200-line traceback when task-logs was fetched. Machine-logs worked because \`/provider_logs\` is already a one-shot JSON endpoint with the same shape.
